content.problemStatement:
A binary string BBB of length NNN is called good, if there exists an integer KKK satisfying the following properties:


1<K<N1 \lt K \lt N1<K<N
KKK divides NNN
Consider a binary grid GGG of dimensions (NK√óK)(\frac{N}{K} \times K)(KN‚Äã√óK), such that its row-major order equals BBB - that is,

for each 1‚â§i‚â§NK1 \leq i \leq \frac{N}{K}1‚â§i‚â§KN‚Äã and 1‚â§j‚â§K1 \leq j \le K1‚â§j‚â§K, we must have Gi,j=B(i‚àí1)‚ãÖK+jG_{i, j} = B_{(i-1)\cdot K + j}Gi,j‚Äã=B(i‚àí1)‚ãÖK+j‚Äã.

Every column of this grid GGG must contain at least one occurrence of 000 and at least one occurrence of 111.
For example,


S=010011S = 010011S=010011 is good, because with K=2K = 2K=2 the resulting grid is [010011]\begin{bmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 \\ 1 & 1\end{bmatrix}‚Äã001‚Äã101‚Äã‚Äã, and both columns contain both 000 and 111.
S=001010S = 001010S=001010 is not good, because with K=2K = 2K=2 the resulting grid is [001010]\begin{bmatrix} 0 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0\end{bmatrix}‚Äã011‚Äã000‚Äã‚Äã and the second column doesn't have any 111's, while with K=3K = 3K=3 the resulting grid is [001010]\begin{bmatrix} 0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 0\end{bmatrix}[00‚Äã01‚Äã10‚Äã] where the first column doesn't have any 111's.

No other choices of KKK exist which divide N=6N = 6N=6, other than 111 and NNN itself (which KKK is not allowed to take), so this string is not good.
A binary string BBB is called beautiful if it contains a non-empty subsequence (not necessarily contiguous) that is good.

You are given a binary string AAA.

Find the length of the longest **contiguous substring** of AAA that's **not** beautiful.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the binary string AAA.
The second line contains the binary string AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, on a new line output the answer: the length of the longest contiguous substring of AAA that's not beautiful.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The whole string is not beautiful.
Test case 222: The string 100110011001 is beautiful, because it is itself good (choose K=2K = 2K=2).
However, the substring 100100100 of length 333 is not beautiful, and hence the answer is 333.
Test case 333: It can be verified that all substrings of 100101010010101001010 with length >4\gt 4>4 are beautiful.
The substring 101010101010 of length 444, formed by the last four characters, is not beautiful - and so the answer is 444.
